description_long: This mosque is a grand, symmetrical structure with four tall, cylindrical minarets at each corner, each topped with ornate domes and finials. The central portion features a large arched gateway flanked by intricately detailed columns and decorative balconies. The facade is adorned with multiple layers of arches and balustrades, giving it a sense of depth and complexity. The building sits on a raised platform with a broad base, and the main entrance is a prominent, pointed archway. The overall color is a light stone or beige, with subtle darker accents highlighting architectural details. The design is reminiscent of the Charminar in Hyderabad, India, showcasing Indo-Islamic architectural elements.
description: A grand mosque with four tall minarets, ornate domes, and a large central arched gateway, featuring intricate Indo-Islamic architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main arched entrance, all four minarets, and the detailed facade with balconies and arches.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the right side minarets and the depth of the balconies and arches, with part of the rear structure visible.
description_view_2: Rear view with the back archway and all four minarets visible, emphasizing the symmetry and raised platform.
description_view_3: Angled rear view showing the left side minarets and the rear entrance, with architectural details on the side walls.
attribute_dome: Four small domes, each atop a minaret, onion-shaped with finials, placed at the corners of the roof; no large central dome visible.
attribute_minaret: Four tall, cylindrical minarets, each rising above the main structure at the corners, with multiple balconies and topped with ornate domes and finials.
attribute_islamic_geometric_patterns: None visible; the structure features architectural ornamentation but no distinct geometric patterns are apparent in the images.
attribute_arabic_calligraphy: None visible; no Arabic calligraphy can be seen on the facade, arches, or domes.
attribute_architectural_style: Indo-Islamic, specifically resembling the Charminar style with its four minarets, arched gateways, and layered balconies.
